req.title: Reset employee password — variant 5
req.statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers: (1) happy-path reset with valid token + 2FA, (2) rejection of an expired token (410), (3) password policy violation (422), (4) boundary at the 30-minute expiry edge, and (5) session invalidation post-reset. Tests are partitioned across EP (valid credentials partition), NEG (expired token, bad password), BVA (token age at 30-min boundary), STC (session state transition), and DT (2FA + policy decision table). Each test is self-contained via fixtures and helper placeholders.

tests.name: test_reset_password_valid_token_and_2fa_succeeds
preconditions: A fresh reset token (age < 30 min) is available
actions: POST /password/reset with valid token, valid new password, and correct 2FA code
Assert old session cookies/tokens are invalidated
expected: All prior sessions for the employee are invalidated
Employee can log in with new password
trace: R-WEB-106: password updated on valid token

tests.name: test_reset_password_expired_token_returns_410
preconditions: A reset token older than 30 minutes exists for an employee
actions: POST /password/reset with the expired token, valid 2FA code, and compliant password
Assert error body indicates token expiry
trace: R-WEB-106: expired links must be rejected
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_reset_password_expired_token_returns_410(
    expired_reset_token, # <fixture> returns token created > 30 min ago
    valid_2fa_code,
    compliant_password
):
    payload = {
        "token": expired_reset_token["token"],
        "new_password": compliant_password,
        "totp_code": valid_2fa_code
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        "http://hr-app/password/reset",
        json=payload
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 410
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error") == "token_expired"

    # Confirm password unchanged by attempting old-password login
    login_resp = <helper_fn>.login(
        expired_reset_token["employee_id"],
        expired_reset_token["original_password"]
    )
    assert login_resp.status_code == 200, "Password must not have changed"

tests.name: test_reset_password_token_at_30_minute_boundary_rejected
preconditions: A reset token aged exactly 30 minutes (1800 seconds) exists
actions: POST /password/reset with a token whose age == 1800 seconds (boundary), valid 2FA, compliant password
Assert the response is 410 (expired at boundary, not before)
expected: HTTP 410 returned at t=1800s (token is not valid at exactly 30 minutes)
trace: R-WEB-106: link expires after 30 minutes — boundary at exact 30-min mark
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_reset_password_token_at_30_minute_boundary_rejected(
    boundary_reset_token, # <fixture> returns token created exactly 1800s ago
    valid_2fa_code,
    compliant_password
):
    payload = {
        "token": boundary_reset_token["token"],
        "new_password": compliant_password,
        "totp_code": valid_2fa_code
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        "http://hr-app/password/reset",
        json=payload
    )
    # At exactly 30 min the token must be considered expired
    assert resp.status_code == 410
    assert resp.json().get("error") == "token_expired"


def test_reset_password_token_just_before_30_minute_boundary_accepted(
    near_boundary_reset_token, # <fixture> token created 1799s ago
    valid_2fa_code,
    compliant_password
):
    payload = {
        "token": near_boundary_reset_token["token"],
        "new_password": compliant_password,
        "totp_code": valid_2fa_code
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        "http://hr-app/password/reset",
        json=payload
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    assert resp.json().get("status") == "password_updated"

tests.name: test_reset_password_policy_violation_returns_422
preconditions: New password does NOT satisfy policy (e.g., too short, no special char)
actions: POST /password/reset with valid token, correct 2FA code, and a non-compliant password
Assert error body details the policy violation
trace: R-WEB-106: 2FA + policy decision table — valid 2FA but bad password
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

@pytest.mark.parametrize("bad_password", [
    "short",          # too short
    "alllowercase1!",  # missing uppercase (example policy rule)
    "NOLOWER1!",       # missing lowercase
    "NoSpecialChar1",  # missing special character
])
def test_reset_password_policy_violation_returns_422(
    fresh_reset_token,
    valid_2fa_code,
    bad_password
):
    payload = {
        "token": fresh_reset_token["token"],
        "new_password": bad_password,
        "totp_code": valid_2fa_code
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        "http://hr-app/password/reset",
        json=payload
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error") == "password_policy_violation"
    assert "violations" in body

tests.name: test_reset_password_sessions_invalidated_after_successful_reset
preconditions: Employee has multiple active sessions (web, mobile)
actions: Capture multiple active session tokens for the employee
POST /password/reset with valid token, 2FA, and compliant password
Assert all captured session tokens are invalidated (401 on subsequent requests)
expected: All pre-existing sessions return 401 on use
System transitions from AUTHENTICATED state to SESSIONS_CLEARED state for all devices
trace: R-WEB-106: all sessions invalidated after password reset

tests.name: test_reset_password_invalid_2fa_code_rejected
actions: POST /password/reset with valid token, WRONG 2FA code, and compliant password
trace: R-WEB-106: 2FA confirmation required — decision table row: valid token + invalid 2FA + valid password
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_reset_password_invalid_2fa_code_rejected(
    fresh_reset_token,
    compliant_password
):
    wrong_totp = "000000"  # statically wrong TOTP value
    payload = {
        "token": fresh_reset_token["token"],
        "new_password": compliant_password,
        "totp_code": wrong_totp
    }
    resp = requests.post(
        "http://hr-app/password/reset",
        json=payload
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (401, 403)
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error") in ("2fa_invalid", "mfa_required")

    # Confirm password unchanged
    login_resp = <helper_fn>.login(
        fresh_reset_token["employee_id"],
        fresh_reset_token["original_password"]
    )
    assert login_resp.status_code == 200, "Password must be unchanged after 2FA failure"
